title: Tapping to See Myself as an Abundant Leader
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the habit of keeping wealth quiet, and stepping into the role of leading from abundance with calm strength
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 81 of a 12-week money programme. The script works with the old tendency to downplay financial success and the worry about standing out, and moves toward a grounded, generous expression of leading from abundance.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 81 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you find yourself minimising financial success in conversation, or when the idea of being seen as someone who leads from abundance feels uncomfortable.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 81 of a 12-week money programme. Week 12 theme: live abundance as normal. Day 81 addresses the habit of keeping wealth quiet and the discomfort of being the person others look to, and works toward leading from abundance with calm, generous strength.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once kept my wealth quiet, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I wasn't sure I wanted to stand out, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I doubted I could lead with money, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I'm ready to inspire others with my example.
Side of eye: I can show what's possible.
Under eye: I lead without arrogance, with calm strength.
Under nose: I share wisdom and encouragement.
Chin: I let my success show the way.
Collarbone: I guide from generosity.
Under arm: I enjoy being a steady, wealthy example.
Top of head: I lead confidently from abundance.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I am an abundant leader and example.
Side of eye: I inspire others to trust their own path.
Under eye: I use my wealth to uplift and guide.
Under nose: I share knowledge with kindness.
Chin: I love how my life encourages others.
Collarbone: I lead with calm, generous strength.
Under arm: I reach many through my abundance.
Top of head: I stand as a confident, steady wealth leader.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 81 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
